title: Rankett.com — Tweet Reaction + Whitelabel Roadmap
html:
TL;DR

  • Yahir saw a tweet that "kinda fucked me up" because it overlapped with Rankett's AI visibility thesis — wants a deep-think on whether someone else is already pursuing the same arc with full intention.
  • Concrete product TODOs: ship a central "Share of Voice" section, record Zoom demo clips, draft pros/cons, gate clients behind legal terms triggered on new-tab open.
  • White label is the wedge: agency partner logos go on the audit tool, email templates, contracts, and the fulfillment-info bot.
  • Need an exportable white-labeled report ("we white-label AI search optimization") as a deliverable surface.
  • Side note: drafting a leave-of-town message to Charlie ("Yo Charlie. Imma be out of town for…"), and "Stepheven" — likely the agency contact thread continuing.

Notes

This note is two things stitched together: an existential gut-check about whether Rankett's AI visibility positioning is defensible, and a workmanlike checklist of the next product moves that would make the white label play real for agency partners.

The tweet-reaction beat is the more interesting half — Yahir explicitly wants the "deep think" on path, possibility, foundation, and "are there people out there rn that are truly pursuing this with all of their intention and soul?" That's the same anxiety that runs through the AI accuracy diligence questions from two months later: is the moat the tech, the speed, or the seriousness?

The product list is small but load-bearing for agency-readiness: the Share of Voice central score, white-labeled exports, agency-logo placement everywhere a client touches, and pre-engagement legal gating. Connects directly to the agency offer template work and to Elijah's role on the team.

Connections

  • rankett — central project.
  • ai visibility engine / share of voice / whitelabel strategy — the three concept pillars touched here.
  • 2025 12 30 agency offer — the offer-template arc; this note's TODOs operationalize it.
  • 2026 03 13 how are they approaching true ai result accuracy — same anxiety, different lens (competitive/diligence).
  • elijah leach — Rankett co-builder.
